fields.label: Personal access tokens
fields.description: Netlify REST API Key. Found here: https://app.netlify.com/user/applications#personal-access-tokens

fields.label: Token
fields.description: Token for accessing the datasource API

instructions.msg: Netlify configuration is stored under a service-keyed jsonData shape: jsonData.services.Netlify.* holds per-service settings (note the service id is capitalized 'Netlify'), while secrets use flat dotted secureJsonData keys of the form "Netlify.<secret>". There is a single service with id 'Netlify'.

Authentication uses a Netlify personal access token (bearer). jsonData.services.Netlify.auth.id is 'bearer_token' (the backend defaults to it) and the token is the write-only secret at secureJsonData key "Netlify.token".

Minimal working payload: {"jsonData":{"services":{"Netlify":{"auth":{"id":"bearer_token"}}}},"secureJsonData":{"Netlify.token":"<netlify-personal-access-token>"}}. Create a token at https://app.netlify.com/user/applications#personal-access-tokens.
Connection: the server URL is fixed at https://api.netlify.com/api/v1 (no server selector and no connection variables).

The secret at secureJsonData key "Netlify.token" is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether it is already configured, read secureJsonFields["Netlify.token"] == true rather than secureJsonData.
